title: Image of an intersection is a subset of the intersection of images
informal_statement: For every function f : alpha -> beta and sets s, t : Set alpha: f '' (s intersect t) is a subset of f '' s intersect f '' t. This is only a subset in general, not an equality -- equality additionally requires f to be injective.
informal_proof_idea: Cited directly via Mathlib's Set.image_inter_subset. Deliberately phrased as the always-true subset statement rather than the false unconditional equality claim (which fails for non-injective f, e.g. f constant on distinct elements of s and t with no common element).
formal_statement_pp: theorem image_inter_subset : ∀ {α β : Type} (f : α → β) (s t : Set α), f '' (s ∩ t) ⊆ f '' s ∩ f '' t

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 6eaaf620), kernel_verified on the first Solve attempt. Companion to image_union.v1 and preimage_inter.v1. This domain's QUEUE.md had flagged the equality version f '' (s ∩ t) = f '' s ∩ f '' t as needing either a counterexample-based negative example or an injectivity caveat since it is false in general; sidestepped that by authoring the always-true subset statement instead, which is the standard Mathlib-aligned formulation (Set.image_inter_subset). Closes the image_inter item in packets/elementary/functions/QUEUE.md.
